channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Sega Cancels Super Game And Shifts Focus From Live Service Games
item.content:encoded:
Venerable video game company Sega is the latest company to come to the realization that there isn't a pot of gold at the end of most paths for Games as a Service titles. Indeed, it its fiscal year end filing it announced that it was cancelling its"Super Game" initiative begun 5 years ago and moved developers away from free to play development over to full game development.

What was Sega's Super Game?

Sega was cagey about what the Super Game was beyond that it was highly ambitious throughout its development. Meant to release by the end of fiscal year 2026 (so... now) it was to be a game that stood "head and shoulders above normal games", perhaps an AAAA game if you would. It was focused on the entire gaming ecosystem, including on the interaction of streamers and their audience at least in how executives talked about it.

The Super Game isn't the only Games as a Service title that Sega has cancelled in recent years. Notably back in 2023, they cancelled the live service shooter Hyenas and restructured their European studios. 

It is important to note that the Super Game initiative is distinct and different from Sega's legacy IP revival despite being announced at the same time. Sega's financials still list New Virtua Fighter Project, Crazy Taxi, Golden Axe, Jet Set Radio, and Streets of Rage as upcoming titles with release dates to be determined.

Sega's Free To Play Struggles Also Highlight Live Service Problems

Sega also announced that several of their free to play mobile games this year had soft performance. Notably Sonic Rumble Party is mentioned as a title that had weak performance, as did one of either Persona 5: The PhantomX or Sega Football Club Champions, as they state that two new titles performed under expectations. Rovio has also struggled during the time, and they have noted a loss based on what they were expecting from the Angry Birds creator. The plan for Rovio according to sega is to continue towards Games as a Service, but only have their own restructuring.

As for Sega's own free-to-play products, they have lowered the priority of work on them. This has already caused the movement of over 100 developers over to 'Full Game' development focusing on mainstay IPs, and we may see more of this in the future.

As for the year as a whole, on gaming Sega reported increased sales, but decreased profitability on the year. They expect that with four new titles based on major IPs this year, and the continued operation of the already existing F2P titles as well as licensing that there will be an increase in both sales and profit for next year, although not as many game sales as we saw ending in 2025.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don
